summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a Dockerfile and documentation, then commit and push the fixes to resolve the conversations on PR #479.
summary.outcome: All three review comments were addressed: upgraded Node to 22 LTS via NodeSource, added npm cache volume, and aligned docs to node:22-alpine. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: Node is installed from Debian packages in the Dockerfile, requiring a NodeSource repository switch to get Node 22 LTS.
The app runs as a 'moltis' user, so created directories (like .npm) must be explicitly owned by that user.
npm cache lives at /home/moltis/.npm and should be declared as a VOLUME for persistence.

code.finding: Node 18.x from Debian was EOL; replaced with Node 22 LTS via NodeSource. Added /home/moltis/.npm VOLUME and ensured directory ownership by moltis user.
Docker example referenced an outdated node version; updated to node:22-alpine to match the Dockerfile.

workflow: PR review comments were fetched directly via GitHub API to triage and prioritize (P1/P2) before making changes.
Conversations should be resolved after committing and pushing fixes.

friction: Node version was mismatched between the Dockerfile and documentation, requiring two separate file edits to stay consistent.
open_items: Verify that PR conversations were actually marked as resolved on GitHub after the push.
Consider pinning the NodeSource Node 22 version to avoid unexpected upgrades in future builds.
